references.title: Demonstrating the impact of study regularity on academic success using learning analytics

extracted.Features: [Weekly Videos and Quizz scores, weekly Last Date of Access to Each Learning Activity]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Data was anonimized and stored in a vector containing the information for the different weeks.
extracted.Cluster Description:
Regular Learners: Often follow lecturer advice, activity closely follows recommended learning pathway.

Irregular or Selective Learners: They pick and choose which activities to partake in. 

Late Learners: Only access VLE closer to exam
extracted.Implications: Students in 2022 were informed of successful and less successful learning pathways undertaken by their previous colleagues. And this translated into a noticeable behaviorable shift relative to previous years - which also translated into a noticeable improvement in the average performance of the students in this cohort.
extracted.Comments: Clustering on this work is done for learning trajectories.
extracted.Limitations:
No ablation study to determine importance of different features.

Data is accumulated weekly (not daily)

Distance metric is not optimized

annotations.text: Flipped classrooms are better suited for learners with developed SRL skills. Authors work focuses on measuring effect of study regularity on academic success. The work defines a measure of study regularity that can uncover learner profiles in B-Learning. This measure heavily correlates with academic success.
Work finishes with a discussion of how this measure of study regularity can be used to help students develop better study habits.
